title: Kabab Koobideh (Ground Meat Kebab)
description: The quintessential Persian street food and restaurant favorite: juicy, perfectly seasoned ground beef and lamb skewers grilled over hot coals.

ingredients.note: Should have a good fat content
Grated and juices completely squeezed out
Ground, bloomed in 1 tbsp hot water

instructions.text: Grate the onions into a fine mesh strainer set over a bowl. Squeeze the grated onions firmly with your hands to remove as much juice as possible. Keep the dry onion pulp and discard the juice.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, ground lamb, dry onion pulp, salt, black pepper, turmeric, and bloomed saffron.
Knead the meat mixture vigorously with your hands for 10-15 minutes until it becomes a cohesive, sticky paste that holds together. This is crucial to prevent the meat from falling off the skewers.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (or overnight) to let the fat firm up and the flavors meld.

Take a handful of the cold meat mixture (about the size of a small apple) and form it into a long cylinder around a wide, flat metal Persian skewer. Press it evenly along the skewer, leaving empty space at both ends. Use your thumb and index finger to create indentations along the meat.
Prepare a charcoal grill (or gas grill) to high heat. Place the skewers on the grill, leaving a gap between the meat and the heat source.
Quickly turn the skewers every 15-20 seconds during the first minute of cooking. This seals the meat on both sides and prevents it from dropping into the fire.
Continue grilling for another 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the meat is browned and cooked through.
Slide the meat off the skewers using a piece of flatbread (lavash) to protect your hands, and sprinkle generously with sumac.

tips: The two main reasons Koobideh falls off the skewer: not squeezing the onion juice out completely, and not kneading the meat long enough to develop the sticky proteins.
storage_notes: Best eaten immediately. Leftovers can be stored in the fridge for up to 3 days and reheated in the microwave, though they will lose some juiciness.
serve_with: Chelo (saffron rice), grilled tomatoes, raw onions, fresh basil, and Doogh (mint yogurt drink).

substitutions.note: If you don't like lamb, use 2 lbs of ground beef, but ensure it is at least 20% fat or the kebab will be dry.
